questions.question: Before computers fit in your pocket, how did the 1940s ENIAC's programming method differ from modern systems?
choices: ENIAC was programmed by rewiring plugboards; modern computers store programs in memory
ENIAC used SSDs; modern computers use vacuum tubes
ENIAC used magnetic tape; modern computers use punch cards
There is no difference in how they are programmed
questions.explanation: ENIAC was not a stored-program computer; you had to move cables around like an old telephone switchboard.

questions.question: In the cloud buzzword bingo, what is the actual distinction between Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) and Software as a Service (SaaS)?
choices: IaaS provides virtualized hardware; SaaS provides ready-to-use applications
IaaS is only for storage; SaaS is only for email
IaaS is managed by the user; SaaS is managed by the ISP
SaaS is a physical server; IaaS is a web browser
questions.explanation: IaaS gives you the server/hardware; SaaS gives you the app (like Gmail). This isn't complicated.
